overview.tagline: A proudly diverse township where cultures, faiths, and communities thrive together
overview.description: The Township of Teaneck is one of Bergen County's largest and most diverse communities, home to nearly 40,000 residents representing a remarkable tapestry of ethnicities, religions, and cultures. Nationally recognized as the first community to voluntarily desegregate its public schools in 1965, Teaneck has a deep tradition of civic engagement and inclusivity. The township features beautiful tree-lined streets, a thriving commercial district along Cedar Lane, excellent schools, and the campus of Fairleigh Dickinson University.
highlights: First community in the nation to voluntarily integrate its public schools (1965)
Remarkably diverse population with vibrant multicultural character
Cedar Lane commercial district with eclectic dining and shopping
Strong residential neighborhoods with tree-lined streets

realEstate.assessmentInfo: Properties are assessed by the Bergen County Tax Assessor's office. Teaneck's large housing stock includes a wide range of home types, from Tudor-style and Colonial homes to split-levels and condominiums.
realEstate.recentTrends: Teaneck's real estate market has been strong, with rising demand driven by the township's diversity, school quality, and proximity to New York City. Properties in the northeast section near the Orthodox Jewish community are particularly competitive. The market features homes across a wide price range, from starter homes to luxury properties.
neighborhoodNotes: Northeast Teaneck — Large Orthodox Jewish community; walkable to synagogues; highly sought-after
West Englewood section — Diverse neighborhood near Englewood border
Cedar Lane area — Walking distance to shops, restaurants, and bus service
Sagamore/Teaneck Road area — Tree-lined streets with mid-century homes
Glenpointe area — Modern condos and apartments near I-95

trashCollection.title: Trash Collection
trashCollection.description: Curbside trash pickup is provided twice weekly by the Township of Teaneck Department of Public Works.
details: Pickup occurs twice per week on designated days by neighborhood zone
Trash must be placed curbside by 6:00 AM on collection day
Use approved containers with secure lids, maximum 50 lbs per container
Bulk item pickup available by appointment through DPW
Holiday schedule adjustments posted on township website

recycling.title: Recycling
recycling.description: Single-stream curbside recycling is collected weekly by the Township.
details: Commingled recyclables collected weekly
Paper and cardboard collected on the same recycling day
Yard waste collected seasonally from April through December
Recycling bins available from the DPW
Electronics and hazardous waste events held periodically

waterSewer.title: Water & Sewer
waterSewer.description: Water is provided by Veolia Water (formerly United Water) and sewer service is maintained by the Township.
details: Water supplied by Veolia Water New Jersey
Quarterly billing for water and sewer service
Sewer maintenance handled by Township DPW
Water quality reports published annually
Emergency water repairs available 24/7

permits.title: Building & Permits
permits.description: The Building Department handles construction permits, zoning approvals, and code enforcement.
details: Building permits required for new construction, additions, and renovations
Zoning applications reviewed by the Planning Board and Board of Adjustment
Permit applications available online and in person at Municipal Building
Inspections scheduled through the Building Department
Historic property considerations in certain neighborhoods

parking.title: Parking
parking.description: The Township operates municipal parking lots and enforces parking regulations, particularly around Cedar Lane and the train station area.
details: Commuter parking available near the bus stops
Metered parking along Cedar Lane business district
Municipal lots on Cedar Lane and Queen Anne Road
Overnight parking restrictions on certain streets
Permit parking zones in residential areas near commercial districts

additionalServices.title: Teaneck Public Library
additionalServices.description: Full-service public library with extensive collections, programs, and community meeting spaces.
details: Located at 840 Teaneck Road
Children's programs, teen events, and adult enrichment
Large collection in multiple languages
Free Wi-Fi, computer access, and study rooms
Part of the Bergen County Cooperative Library System (BCCLS)

additionalServices.title: Recreation Department
additionalServices.description: Year-round recreational programs for residents of all ages, including the Teaneck Swim Club.
details: Youth sports leagues, summer camps, and after-school programs
Adult fitness classes and senior programs
Votee Park facilities including pool, tennis, and sports fields
Online registration through Community Pass

noise.title: Noise Ordinance
noise.summary: Teaneck enforces noise regulations to maintain quality of life in its residential neighborhoods.
keyRules: Construction noise prohibited before 7:00 AM and after 7:00 PM on weekdays
Saturday construction allowed 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM only
No construction noise permitted on Sundays or federal holidays
Leaf blowers and power equipment restricted to certain hours
Amplified music and loud gatherings subject to noise limits after 10:00 PM

propertyMaintenance.title: Property Maintenance
propertyMaintenance.summary: Property owners are required to maintain their properties in good condition, including lawns, structures, and sidewalks.
keyRules: Lawns must be maintained and grass kept at a reasonable height
Property owners responsible for sidewalk snow removal within 24 hours after snowfall
Fences must not exceed 6 feet in rear yards and 4 feet in front yards
Abandoned or inoperable vehicles must be removed from property
Property exteriors including paint, gutters, and roofs must be maintained

pets.title: Pet Regulations
pets.summary: Teaneck requires dog licensing and enforces leash laws throughout the township.
keyRules: All dogs over 7 months must be licensed annually with proof of rabies vaccination
Dogs must be leashed on all public property
Pet waste must be picked up immediately by the owner
Maximum number of pets per household may apply
No exotic or dangerous animals permitted as pets

parking.title: Parking Regulations
parking.summary: Street parking is regulated throughout the township with specific rules for residential and commercial areas.
keyRules: No overnight parking on township streets between 2:00 AM and 6:00 AM without a permit
Metered parking enforced along Cedar Lane and commercial streets
Snow emergency parking bans enforced — vehicles must be off-street
Commercial vehicles prohibited from parking on residential streets overnight
Residential permit parking required near some commercial zones

rentals.title: Rental Property Regulations
rentals.summary: Rental properties in Teaneck must be registered and comply with housing codes.
keyRules: Rental properties must be registered with the Township
Certificate of Habitability required before occupancy of rental units
Landlords must maintain properties to housing code standards
Multi-family dwellings subject to fire safety inspections
Short-term rentals (under 30 days) are regulated

other.title: Eruv Boundaries
other.summary: Teaneck permits and maintains an eruv, a symbolic boundary important to the Orthodox Jewish community for Sabbath observance.
keyRules: Eruv boundaries are maintained by the local community organizations
Township permits the use of utility poles and structures for eruv wires
Eruv status is published weekly for the community
Maintenance coordination with utility companies as needed
other.source: Township of Teaneck Code / Community organizations

other.title: Tree Preservation
other.summary: Teaneck protects significant trees on public and private property.
keyRules: Permit required to remove trees over a specified diameter on private property
Replacement planting may be required when trees are removed
Shade Tree Advisory Board oversees public tree management
Damage to public trees subject to fines

publicSchools.name: Teaneck High School
publicSchools.notes: Comprehensive high school known for its diverse student body, strong academics, and extensive extracurricular programs

publicSchools.name: Thomas Jefferson Middle School
publicSchools.notes: Middle school serving students across the township

publicSchools.name: Benjamin Franklin Middle School
publicSchools.notes: Middle school providing comprehensive academics and extracurriculars

publicSchools.name: Hawthorne Elementary School
publicSchools.notes: Elementary school in the northeast section of the township

publicSchools.name: Lowell Elementary School
publicSchools.notes: Elementary school in the central part of the township

publicSchools.name: Whittier Elementary School
publicSchools.notes: Elementary school serving the southern portion of Teaneck

publicSchools.name: Longfellow Elementary School
publicSchools.notes: Elementary school in the western section of the township

privateSchools.name: Yeshivat He'Atid
privateSchools.notes: Modern Orthodox day school with dual curriculum

privateSchools.name: Ma'ayanot Yeshiva High School for Girls
privateSchools.notes: All-girls Modern Orthodox yeshiva high school

privateSchools.name: Torah Academy of Bergen County (TABC)
privateSchools.notes: All-boys Modern Orthodox yeshiva high school

privateSchools.name: The Moriah School
privateSchools.notes: Orthodox Jewish day school with comprehensive dual curriculum

nearbyHigherEd: Fairleigh Dickinson University (Teaneck campus) — in Teaneck
Hackensack Meridian School of Medicine — in Nutley, 20 minutes

whyMoveHere: Affordable by Bergen County standards with a wide range of housing options
Strong public schools with history of academic achievement
Vibrant Cedar Lane shopping and dining district
Excellent bus service to New York City

commuteToNYC.duration: 30-50 minutes to Port Authority Bus Terminal / George Washington Bridge
commuteToNYC.notes: Teaneck has excellent bus service to Manhattan with multiple NJ Transit routes running along Teaneck Road, Cedar Lane, and Route 4. The commute to the Port Authority Bus Terminal is approximately 30-50 minutes. Access to I-95/Route 4 provides a direct driving route to the George Washington Bridge and Upper Manhattan.

costOfLiving.summary: Teaneck offers a moderate cost of living for Bergen County, with a wide range of housing from modest to upscale, making it accessible to families at various income levels.
costOfLiving.vsStateAverage: Approximately 25% above the New Jersey state average

pros: Rich cultural and ethnic diversity with strong community bonds
Affordable housing options across a wide price range
Excellent bus access to NYC with multiple routes
Strong commitment to public education and civic engagement
cons: High property taxes relative to home values
No direct NJ Transit rail service
Some areas experience traffic congestion on Route 4
School rankings vary; some families prefer private options
Older housing stock may require updates

restaurants.name: Veggie Heaven
restaurants.description: Popular Asian-inspired vegetarian and vegan restaurant offering creative dishes in a casual setting.

restaurants.name: Mocha Roti Bar
restaurants.description: Caribbean-inspired restaurant known for roti wraps, jerk chicken, and tropical flavors.

restaurants.name: Chopstix
restaurants.description: Kosher Chinese restaurant popular with the local community for takeout and dine-in Chinese-American fare.

restaurants.name: Mango Mango
restaurants.description: Tropical-themed dessert and smoothie shop known for fresh fruit creations and shaved ice.

restaurants.name: Keter Steakhouse
restaurants.description: Upscale kosher steakhouse offering prime cuts, elegant ambiance, and a sophisticated dining experience.

parks.name: Votee Park
parks.description: Teaneck's largest park featuring a swimming pool, tennis courts, basketball courts, sports fields, playgrounds, and a walking track. The hub of township recreation.

parks.name: Andreas Park
parks.description: Neighborhood park with playground equipment, open fields, and shaded picnic areas.

parks.name: Phelps Park
parks.description: Community park featuring baseball fields, walking paths, and green space along the Hackensack River.

parks.name: Sagamore Park
parks.description: Quiet neighborhood park with playground, benches, and open green space popular with families.

events.name: Teaneck International Day
events.description: Annual celebration of Teaneck's cultural diversity featuring food, music, dance, and performances from cultures around the world.

events.name: Cedar Lane Street Fair
events.description: Popular street fair along Cedar Lane with vendors, food, live entertainment, and family activities.
events.when: Spring and Fall, typically May and September

events.name: Teaneck Summer Concert Series
events.description: Free outdoor concerts held in township parks throughout the summer months.

events.name: Memorial Day Parade
events.description: Annual parade through town honoring veterans and fallen service members.
events.when: Memorial Day, last Monday in May

attractions.name: Cedar Lane Commercial District
attractions.description: Teaneck's main shopping and dining street featuring eclectic restaurants, shops, and services reflecting the town's diverse character.

attractions.name: Fairleigh Dickinson University
attractions.description: Private university campus in Teaneck featuring the Becton Theater, Rothman Center for athletics events, and beautiful grounds open to visitors.

attractions.name: Teaneck Public Library
attractions.description: Modern public library offering extensive programs, collections, and community spaces.

shoppingAreas: Cedar Lane — Main commercial corridor with diverse dining and shops
Teaneck Road — Retail and services along a major north-south route
DeGraw Avenue — Kosher restaurants and shops in the northeast section
Route 4 corridor — Larger retail stores and shopping centers

government.meetingSchedule: Township Council meets the 1st and 3rd Tuesday of each month at 8:00 PM
government.meetingLocation: Municipal Building, 818 Teaneck Road, Teaneck, NJ 07666

importantNumbers.label: Bergen County Office of Emergency Management
